title: Commute-Time Hijack with Geographically Impossible Terminal Placement
narrative: Fraudster exploits cardholder's predictable routine by timing transactions during their known commute window but from a terminal 200+ miles away. Victim typically makes $4-12 gas or coffee purchases between 7:15-7:45 AM on weekdays at consistent locations (dist1 < 15km from addr1). Attack pattern shows $87-$340 transactions at 7:23 AM on Tuesday and 7:31 AM on Thursday at electronics retailers (MCC 5732) where dist1 = 340-890km. Fraudster relies on behavioral velocity systems trained on time-of-day patterns to ignore the transaction, assuming commute activity is low-risk. Card present with correct PIN suggests skimming or card duplication. Prior 60-day history shows card1_txn_count_7to9am averaging 8-11 transactions monthly, all within 25km radius. Attack transactions match temporal profile perfectly but spatial profile is impossible without supersonic travel.
indicators: Transaction occurs within victim's established commute window (Â±15 minutes of historical 7:15-7:45 AM pattern)
dist1 exceeds 200km from addr1 despite transaction timestamp matching routine local purchases
MCC shift from typical commute codes (5541 gas, 5814 fast food) to high-value electronics (5732) or jewelry (5944)
TransactionAmt $87-$340 vs. historical commute average $4-12 over prior 90 days
card1_txn_count_7to9am shows consistent monthly pattern (8-11 txns) but all prior dist1 < 25km
Card-present with PIN despite geographically impossible location given time constraints
No corresponding travel indicators: no airport MCC codes, no hotel charges, no fuel purchases along route in preceding 24 hours
summary: Card-present transactions occur during the cardholder's normal commute hours (7:15-7:45 AM) but at terminals 200+ miles away, with purchase amounts jumping from typical $4-12 coffee or gas to $87-$340 electronics, all while maintaining the victim's established time-of-day pattern.
reasoning: The fraudster uses a skimmed or cloned card with PIN to exploit velocity systems that recognize familiar transaction times as low-risk, betting that temporal matching will override spatial impossibility checks—the cardholder cannot physically travel 200+ miles during their morning routine without any supporting travel transactions.
indicator_explanations: Purchase happens exactly when the cardholder normally commutes to work
Transaction location is hundreds of miles from cardholder's home address
Merchant type suddenly shifts from coffee shops to expensive electronics stores
Purchase amount is thirty times larger than typical morning spending
Morning transaction pattern is steady but always near home until now
Chip and PIN were used despite physical impossibility of cardholder presence
No plane tickets, hotels, or gas stations suggest legitimate travel occurred
